methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Skeleton Technologies is a Series E climate and energy company founded by Taavi Madiberk that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation universe spans 21 same-sector peers, including 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private companies such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, direct same-stage peers with sufficient data depth remain limited.

Given data constraints, stage-progression represents the applicable triangulation method. Using total capital raised of $200M as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier for progression within the Series E window yields an implied valuation of $280M. This method assumes that capital raised correlates meaningfully with valuation trajectory at this stage and that the standard multiplier holds for frontier-energy companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $238M (low) to $322M (median $280M), centered on the stage-progression result. The primary constraint is the absence of a sufficiently deep peer-median dataset; the Climate & Energy sector contains measurable comparables, but same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints with sufficient similarity do not meet the minimum threshold of three observations. Key uncertainties include technology maturity relative to stage peers, cash burn profile, and market adoption timelines—factors not resolved by capital-raised proxies alone. Additional comparable data or forward revenue visibility would materially sharpen this range.
